2605.16828.title: Prediction-Intervention Games and Invariant Sets
2605.16828.paper_summary: We consider the following two-player game: using observational data, the leader chooses a prediction function for a response variable $Y$ from given covariates. The follower then reacts with an intervention on some covariates in the underlying structural causal model to maximize their own objective. The leader knows the intervention targets, but may have limited knowledge of the follower's objective. We call this setup a prediction-intervention game, a special case of a Stackelberg game. Finding an optimal strategy for the leader is generally difficult. To avoid severe performance loss, the leader may base their prediction on the causal parents of $Y$, or more generally on an invariant subset of covariates. We prove, for two common classes of follower objectives, that predictors based on the stable blanket, a specific invariant subset, are always better or as good as those based on the causal parents. We further upper bound the leader's post-intervention risk by a worst-case risk over allowed interventions and strengthen existing distribution generalization results to analyze this bound: we give sufficient conditions under which stable-blanket predictors are worst-case optimal, and show by examples that these conditions cannot in general be dropped. Finally, we discuss practical strategies for settings with known and unknown graph, and test them on simulated and real-world data.
2605.16828.paper_summary_zh: 我們考慮以下的雙人遊戲：領導者使用觀察數據，從給定的協變量中選擇一個對應變量 $Y$ 的預測函數。然後，跟隨者對潛在結構因果模型中的某些協變量進行干預，以最大化他們自己的目標。領導者知道干預的目標，但可能對跟隨者的目標了解有限。我們稱這種設置為預測-干預遊戲，這是一種特殊的斯塔克爾伯格遊戲。找到領導者的最佳策略通常是困難的。為了避免嚴重的性能損失，領導者可以基於 $Y$ 的因果父母，或者更一般地基於不變的協變量子集來進行預測。我們證明，對於兩種常見的跟隨者目標，基於穩定毛毯（一個特定的不變子集）的預測器總是比基於因果父母的預測器更好或至少一樣好。我們進一步通過允許的干預上界領導者的干預後風險，並加強現有的分佈泛化結果來分析這個界限：我們給出穩定毛毯預測器在最壞情況下最佳的充分條件，並通過例子展示這些條件在一般情況下不能被省略。最後，我們討論了在已知和未知圖的設置中的實用策略，並在模擬和真實數據上進行測試。
2605.16828.author: Linus Kühne et.al.
2605.16828.authors: Linus Kühne, Felix Schur, Jonas Peters
